explanation: The property captures the requirement: In PCV Mode, during the expiratory phase, if a sudden pressure drop above the inhale trigger sensitivity is detected (i.e., dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV becomes true), then the ventilator should trigger the inspiratory phase at the next time step. This is modeled by the implication: if PCVMode & expiratoryPhase & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV are true, then X(inspiratoryPhase) should be true. By using template 41, we ensure the correct condition and timing for the system's response to the spontaneous breath trigger.

The requirement states that, in PCV mode and during the expiratory phase (within the trigger window), if there is a spontaneous breath detected (which amounts to detecting a sudden drop in airway pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity, i.e., 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV'), then the ventilator should trigger a new inspiration phase (here denoted as 'inspiratoryPhaseStart'). The correct way to model this causality is to say: Whenever we are in PCV mode (PCVMode), in the expiratory phase (expiratoryPhase), and a drop in pressure is detected ('dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV'), it should imply that the inspiratory phase starts in the next instant. Thus, we need to use a template that allows expressing (Condition -> (X Action)) under all circumstances. Therefore, we select template 41: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), with: bool_exp1 = PCVMode, bool_exp2 = (expiratoryPhase & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV), and bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPhaseStart. N_DURATION is not required.

The natural language states that in PCV Mode, within the expiratory phase trigger window, when a spontaneous drop in pressure is detected above the inhale trigger sensitivity for PCV mode (drop(PAW) > ITS_PCV), the ventilator should trigger a new breathing (inspiration) cycle. The chosen atomic proposition 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV' directly encodes the detection of the relevant pressure drop, and 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' indicates the start of the inspiratory phase (a new breathing cycle is triggered). Thus, whenever (globally) in PCV Mode during the expiratory phase, if 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV' is true, on the next step 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' must hold (i.e., the ventilator should trigger a new inspiratory-breathing cycle). This is best captured by the template G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)), using 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV' as bool_exp2 and 'inspiratoryPhaseStart' as bool_exp3. N_DURATION is not relevant for this template, so set to null.

The property describes that whenever the system is in PCV mode (PCVMode is true), in the expiratory phase (expiratoryPhase is true), and a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity in PCV mode is detected (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV is true), then in the next step (i.e., immediately after), the inspiratory phase must be activated (inspiratoryPhase is true). This directly reflects the requirement that a spontaneous breathing event during expiration triggers a new breathing cycle. N_DURATION is set to 1 as the requirement dictates an immediate transition after the detection.

The natural language specifies that in PCV Mode, within the expiratory phase's trigger window, when a sudden pressure drop above the trigger threshold (drop(PAW) > ITS_PCV, indicated as dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV) is detected (i.e., spontaneous breathing), the ventilator should initiate a new inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhase becomes active). This is a causality: if during expiratoryPhase and the trigger condition dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV occurs, then the system must move to inspiratoryPhase at the next step. We encode this using: G ((expiratoryPhase & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV) -> (X inspiratoryPhase)). This LTL ensures that whenever the trigger occurs during expiration, the next state is inspiration, which captures the breath triggering mechanism as described.

The requirement states that, in PCV mode, during the expiratory phase, if (within the trigger window) a sudden drop in airway pressure greater than the inhale trigger sensitivity (i.e., dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV) is observed, a new inspiration phase shall be triggered (i.e., inspiratoryPhaseStart). This can be captured by requiring that whenever PCVMode is active AND expiratoryPhase is active AND dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV becomes true, then (in the next transition) inspiratoryPhaseStart should occur. Template 41 encodes: Globally, whenever (PCVMode AND expiratoryPhase AND dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV) is true, in the next step, inspiratoryPhaseStart must be true. N_DURATION is not necessary in this template.

The property expresses that, globally (at all times), if the system is in PCV mode (PCVMode) and it is currently the expiratory phase (expiratoryPhase is true), and if a drop in airway pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity occurs (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV is true), then in the next step the inspiratory phase should begin (inspiratoryPhase should become active). This directly models the requirement: "Within the trigger window during the expiratory phase, in the case of spontaneous breathing, the ventilator shall trigger a new breathing cycle (goes to inspiration phase) when it detects a sudden drop in pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity in PCV mode." The boolean expression for the trigger condition combines 'PCVMode', 'expiratoryPhase', and 'dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV', and the effect is activating 'inspiratoryPhase' in the next step.

The natural language requirement says: During PCV mode, if in expiratory phase, and a sudden pressure drop above inhale trigger sensitivity (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV) is detected (which means spontaneous breath attempt), the ventilator must begin a new inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhaseStart). This is a typical reactivity property: whenever that conjunction occurs, the next state should trigger inspiratoryPhaseStart. We encode this as: G ((PCVMode & expiratoryPhase & dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV) -> X inspiratoryPhaseStart), which is template 41. All boolean expressions now contain valid expressions only.

The requirement specifies that, in PCV mode, during the expiratory phase, if a sudden drop in airway pressure above the inspiratory trigger sensitivity for PCV mode (dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV) is detected (which indicates a patient is trying to breathe), then the ventilator should immediately trigger the next breathing cycle, i.e., start the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseStart). To capture this causality, we can use the template 'G (A -> X B)' which means: whenever A (the trigger condition: PCV mode active, expiratory phase active, and dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV detected) is true, then in the next time step B (the start of the inspiration phase) must be true. (Immediate reaction is typical in ventilator logic.)

Bool_exp2 corresponds to PCV mode AND expiratory phase active AND dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV, and bool_exp3 is inspiratoryPhaseStart.

Template 11 fits best for this cause-effect in next cycle.

The requirement states that in PCV Mode, during the expiratory phase, if there is a sudden drop in airway pressure above the inhale trigger sensitivity (indicated by dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV), a new inspiration phase (inspiratoryPhase) must be triggered. This matches a safety-critical causality: whenever the system is in PCVMode, in the expiratoryPhase, and detects dropPAW_greaterThan_ITS_PCV, it must initiate inspiratoryPhase in the next step. This is naturally expressed as a global property using a 'next' temporal operator, so template 11 ('G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3))') is chosen. Here, bool_exp2 captures that we are in PCV mode, in the expiratory phase, and have detected the trigger sensitivity drop, and bool_exp3 indicates the inspiratory phase is active in the following step. This template most directly fits the medical requirement for a system reaction on the next cycle.
